question: Can a dental hygienist licensed in another U.S. state obtain a Tennessee dental hygiene license without re-taking the clinical exam?
ideal_answer: Yes, by criteria approval (reciprocity). Per Tennessee Board of Dentistry Rule 0460-03-.02, a hygienist licensed in another state may obtain Tennessee licensure by submitting evidence of the degree, a current passport-style photograph, evidence of good moral character, and at least two letters from dentists attesting to character and ability. The applicant must never have failed any Board-approved examination under Rule 0460-03-.05 unless they subsequently retook and passed it. Authority: T.C.A. §§ 4-5-202, 4-5-204, 63-5-105, 63-5-109, 63-5-114.

question: Does Alabama require a real estate licensee from another state to complete additional Alabama-specific coursework before getting a reciprocal license?
ideal_answer: Yes. Per Alabama Real Estate Commission Rule 790-X-1-.18, reciprocal applicants must complete a six (6) hour course in Alabama real estate law approved by the Commission and pass the Alabama portion of the licensing examination. The certification of current licensure from the other state must have been issued within 120 days of AREC's receipt of the reciprocal application. Authority: Code of Ala. 1975, §§ 34-27-32(a), 34-27-32(b), 34-27-35.

question: How can a dentist who holds a license in another state obtain a Special Purpose License to practice across state lines into Alabama?
ideal_answer: Per Alabama Board of Dental Examiners Rule 270-X-2-.18, the Board issues a Special Purpose License to practice dentistry across state lines only to applicants whose principal practice and license are in a state whose laws permit issuance of a similar reciprocal license to Alabama dentists. The applicant must hold a current full license in good standing in the home state, have no disciplinary actions, and meet Board application requirements. Authority: Code of Ala. 1975, § 34-9-10.

question: What does Mississippi require of a non-resident real estate broker applicant from a state without a reciprocity agreement?
ideal_answer: Per Mississippi Real Estate Commission rules in 30 Miss. Admin. Code Pt. 1601, Ch. 1, the non-resident applicant must qualify in all respects — education, background investigation, examination, and fees — the same as a Mississippi resident, except for the residency requirement. The applicant must furnish a certification from the executive officer of the home-state real estate commission and file an irrevocable consent to service of process in Mississippi. Authority: Miss. Code Ann. §§ 73-35-7, 73-35-35.

question: Under Georgia rules, can an out-of-state real estate licensee obtain a Georgia license without taking the Georgia examination?
ideal_answer: Yes, by reciprocity, but only if the home state extends Georgia licensees the same treatment. Per Georgia Real Estate Commission Rule 520-1, an out-of-state applicant licensed in another state may be licensed in Georgia without examination upon submission of the appropriate forms and a certification from the home-state licensing body showing the license is in good standing and that the applicant passed a state-administered real estate examination. Authority: O.C.G.A. § 43-40-9; O.C.G.A. § 43-40-25.

question: What requirements does Texas impose on a dental hygienist seeking licensure by credentials from another U.S. state?
ideal_answer: Per 22 TAC § 103.3, an applicant for Texas dental hygiene licensure by credentials must hold a current license in good standing from another U.S. state, district, or territory; demonstrate a minimum of three years of active clinical practice as a licensed dental hygienist; and meet the general qualifications for licensure under § 103.1 and the continuing education requirements of Chapter 104. Authority: 22 TAC Chapter 103.

question: What is the biennial renewal fee for a Mississippi dental hygienist's local-anesthesia administration certification?
ideal_answer: $25.00 per the biennial renewal cycle, concurrent with the dental hygiene license renewal. Per Mississippi State Board of Dental Examiners Rule 1.13 (Board Regulation 13), the certification to administer local anesthesia is renewed every two years for a $25.00 fee. Authority: Miss. Code Ann. § 73-9-5.

question: What is the original license fee and what is the renewal fee for a Tennessee real estate broker or affiliate broker?
ideal_answer: Per Tennessee Real Estate Commission Rule 1260-01-.12, the fee for issuance of an original license is ninety dollars ($90.00) and each renewal of a license requires a fee of seventy-five dollars ($75.00). Late renewals carry a fifty dollar ($50.00) penalty per month or portion thereof. Authority: T.C.A. §§ 62-13-203, 62-13-208, 62-13-307, 62-13-308, 62-13-319.

question: By when does an Arkansas real estate broker or salesperson license expire each year, and by when must the renewal be filed?
ideal_answer: Per Arkansas Real Estate Commission Rule 6.1, every real estate broker or salesperson license expires on December 31 of each calendar year unless renewed. Renewal applications accompanied by the required fee must be filed with the Commission no later than September 30. Authority: A.C.A. § 17-42-307(c).

question: What is the renewal fee and timeline for an Arkansas educational academic license issued to a physician?
ideal_answer: Per Arkansas State Medical Board Rule 28, an educational academic license is valid for one year and requires renewal with a $220.00 fee. Licensees must reapply, and the physician may be required to appear before the Board with their supervisor. After two years of practice under the educational academic license, the physician may qualify for an unrestricted license. Authority: Ark. Code Ann. § 17-95-201 et seq.

question: What is the fee for an Alabama Physician Assistant license application, and what is the annual license renewal fee?
ideal_answer: Per Alabama Board of Medical Examiners Rules 540-X-7-.06 and 540-X-7-.07, the application fee for Physician Assistant licensure is $200, with waiver provisions for certain certified individuals. The rule also establishes the annual license renewal fee that PAs must pay to maintain licensure. Authority: Code of Ala. 1975, §§ 34-24-290 et seq.

question: Compare the original/renewal fee structure for a real estate license in Tennessee versus Arkansas. Which state charges more for an initial broker license?
ideal_answer: Tennessee charges more for original issuance. Per Tennessee Real Estate Commission Rule 1260-01-.12, the issuance of an original license is $90 and each renewal is $75. Arkansas Real Estate Commission Regulation 3.1 (and updated fee schedule in Rule 235.10.22-002F-22677) sets specific application, renewal, and transfer fees that historically have been lower than Tennessee's $90 original-issuance fee. Both states impose late penalties for lapsed renewals.

question: How does the dental hygienist application fee in Tennessee compare with the dentist annual registration fee in Alabama?
ideal_answer: These are governed by different rules and indexed separately. Tennessee Board of Dentistry Rule 0460-01-.02 sets the schedule for application, renewal, reinstatement, duplicate license, and examination fees for dental hygienists. Alabama Board of Dental Examiners Rule 270-X-2-.07 governs the annual registration of dentists in Alabama including fee, deadlines, and consequences for non-compliance. Direct dollar comparison must be drawn from each rule's fee table.

question: Among the states that fund a real estate Recovery Fund through licensee fees, what fees do Alabama and Tennessee assess?
ideal_answer: Per Alabama Real Estate Commission Rule 790-X-2 (sections .20-.23), Alabama assesses Recovery Fund fees with exemptions for inactive licenses (collected only when activated). Per Tennessee Real Estate Commission Rule 1260-01-.12, Tennessee charges a $1 per-license fee for deposit into the real estate education and recovery account at original issuance and renewal. The Alabama fund's exemption for inactive licensees is a structural difference from Tennessee's flat per-license deposit.

question: Compare Mississippi's notary commission cost components (application fee plus bond) with Alabama's real estate Recovery Fund fee structure.
ideal_answer: These instruments are not directly comparable in dollar amount but answer different policy questions. Per Mississippi Secretary of State Notary Public regulations (Title 1, Part 5), the notary applicant must pay a $25 application fee and post a $5,000 surety bond before the commission becomes effective; the bond is not a fee paid to the State. Per Alabama Real Estate Commission Rule 790-X-2-.20-.23, a Recovery Fund fee is assessed at licensure, with exemptions for inactive licensees. The MS notary regime requires private bonding capacity; the AL real estate regime pools licensee fees into a state-administered recovery fund.

question: How does the Mississippi securities registration fee structure compare with Arkansas's real estate timeshare registration fee structure?
ideal_answer: Per Mississippi Securities Act Rule 4.03 (30 Miss. Admin. Code Pt. 4), Mississippi charges initial registration, re-registration, and termination fees for securities offerings, with separate notice-filing fees for federal covered securities. Per Arkansas Real Estate Commission Rule 13.1 (as amended by Act 733 of 2021), Arkansas charges registration, renewal, and bond-requirement fees for time-share plans and associated agents. Both regimes use a tiered fee structure but apply to entirely different products (securities vs. timeshare interests).

question: When were the rules in Tennessee Board of Medical Examiners Chapter 0880-10 transferred, and to which chapter were they moved?
ideal_answer: The rules in Chapter 0880-10 were transferred to Chapter 1130-02 (Rules of the Committee on Physician Assistants) effective August 16, 2021, pursuant to Public Chapter 565 of 2021. The transfer covered rules 0880-10-.01 through 0880-10-.19. Authority: T.C.A. §§ 4-5-202, 4-5-204, 63-1-107, 63-19-104, 63-19-201, 63-19-206, 63-19-209.

question: When was Texas State Board of Dental Examiners Rule §117.1 (regarding dental faculty license) most recently amended?
ideal_answer: Per the adoption-and-amendment history in 22 TAC § 117.1, the rule was originally adopted in 1976 and has been amended multiple times. The most recent amendment recorded in the rule's history was effective March 23, 2015. Authority: 22 TAC Part 5 (State Board of Dental Examiners).

question: When did the Arkansas Real Estate Commission propose its bundle of amendments adding automatic licensure for uniformed service members and fee waivers for low-income applicants?
ideal_answer: Per the Arkansas Register filing 235.10.22 (notices of proposed rule), the AREC proposed amendments to Rules 4.5, 6.1, 11.1 (automatic licensure for uniformed service members, veterans, and their spouses), Rule 3.4 (fee waivers for low-income applicants), and related rules under authority of Acts 135, 725, 733, and 746 of 2021. The proposals were the subject of public hearings in late 2022. The final rules (235.10.22-003F, etc.) carried the amendments into effect.

question: When was Alabama Board of Medical Examiners Chapter 540-X-12 (Medical Examiners) initially filed, and when was its most recent amendment?
ideal_answer: Per the administrative history in Chapter 540-X-12, the rule was initially filed in June 2019 and amended in February 2021. The chapter governs Alabama Board of Medical Examiners rules; subsequent amendments and repeals to specific appendices (e.g., 540-X-12-AA) followed in February 2021. Authority: Code of Ala. 1975, §§ 34-24-293, 34-24-298, 34-24-299, 34-24-303, 34-24-306.

question: What statute grants the Mississippi State Board of Medical Licensure authority to promulgate rules governing medical practice?
ideal_answer: Miss. Code Ann. § 73-43-11 (1972, as amended) grants the Board the power to promulgate and publish reasonable rules and regulations necessary to discharge its functions and enforce the laws regulating the practice of medicine. The Board may not adopt any rule that conflicts with the prohibitions in Miss. Code Ann. § 73-49-3. The MSBML cites this section as authority for its administrative rules in Title 30, Part 2635 et seq. of the Mississippi Administrative Code.

question: Under what Tennessee statutes does the Board of Medical Examiners derive authority to convene screening panels and conduct informal hearings?
ideal_answer: Per Tennessee Board of Medical Examiners Rule 0880-02-.11, the authority for screening panels established by the Board to conduct informal hearings, make settlement recommendations, and finalize settlements derives from T.C.A. § 63-6-214(i). The Board's broader rulemaking authority for Chapter 0880-02 is T.C.A. §§ 4-5-202, 4-5-204, 63-6-101, 63-6-105, 63-6-207, 63-6-210, and Chapter 33, Public Acts of 1999.

question: What is the statutory authority for the Mississippi Real Estate Commission, and how is the Commission constituted?
ideal_answer: Per Mississippi Real Estate Commission Rule 1.1 (30 Miss. Admin. Code Pt. 1601, Ch. 1) and Miss. Code Ann. § 73-35-5, the MREC is established as a five-member body appointed by the Governor with Senate approval. Each appointee must meet residency and professional experience requirements, including six years of Mississippi residency. Authority: Miss. Code Ann. § 73-35-5.

question: What examinations must a candidate pass to obtain a Georgia dental license, and what is the passing score for the jurisprudence component?
ideal_answer: Per Georgia Board of Dentistry Rule 150-3-.01, a candidate for dental licensure must pass the National Board Theory Examinations (Part I and II) or the Integrated National Board Dental Exam, plus a jurisprudence examination on Georgia laws and rules with a score of 75 or higher. The jurisprudence exam may be taken open-book, and the score is valid for one (1) year. Clinical examination requirements are also set out in Rule 150-3-.01.

question: Which examinations does the Mississippi State Board of Dental Examiners accept for dental hygiene licensure by examination?
ideal_answer: Per Mississippi State Board of Dental Examiners Rule 1.14 (30 Miss. Admin. Code Pt. 2301), an applicant for dental hygiene licensure by examination who graduated from an ADA-CODA-accredited school must successfully complete the National Board Dental Hygiene Examinations of the Joint Commission on National Dental Examinations. The Board may accept national board certification in lieu of a written examination but retains the right to administer additional written and practical examinations as it deems necessary. Authority: Miss. Code Ann. § 73-9-23.
